item.title: Sex Offenders, PAC, NHI, Independence
item.ns0:title: Sex Offenders, PAC, NHI, Independence
item.description:
Tonight on Our News, the Bahamas Crisis Center’s chief weighs in on the heated debate over whether the national sex offender registry should be made public, while the Opposition Leader pushes for a major shift in transparency for parliamentary proceedings. We investigate the tragic death of another visitor in local waters and the growing alarm over delayed National Health Insurance payments that may be impacting patient care. Our team also explores the government’s new digital modernization strategy and examines striking new census data regarding the disability landscape in the country. Stay tuned for these headlines, a preview of upcoming independence celebrations, and your full tropical weather outlook.
-----
Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns1:encoded:
Tonight on Our News, the Bahamas Crisis Center’s chief weighs in on the heated debate over whether the national sex offender registry should be made public, while the Opposition Leader pushes for a major shift in transparency for parliamentary proceedings. We investigate the tragic death of another visitor in local waters and the growing alarm over delayed National Health Insurance payments that may be impacting patient care. Our team also explores the government’s new digital modernization strategy and examines striking new census data regarding the disability landscape in the country. Stay tuned for these headlines, a preview of upcoming independence celebrations, and your full tropical weather outlook.
-----

item.ns0:summary:
Tonight on Our News, the Bahamas Crisis Center’s chief weighs in on the heated debate over whether the national sex offender registry should be made public, while the Opposition Leader pushes for a major shift in transparency for parliamentary proceedings. We investigate the tragic death of another visitor in local waters and the growing alarm over delayed National Health Insurance payments that may be impacting patient care. Our team also explores the government’s new digital modernization strategy and examines striking new census data regarding the disability landscape in the country. Stay tuned for these headlines, a preview of upcoming independence celebrations, and your full tropical weather outlook.
